The general destructive-confirm quality is solidly evidenced -- but because engine-b was never actually prod-tagged (see R-SAFE-03 row), no INCREMENTAL prod-specific confirmation step was ever triggered to observe, so the 'demanded from you because this is production' half of the tester's own framing is not independently verifiable this run.

Zero Sev1 message-style violations in M6's pooled corpus. One recurring Sev2 theme (already tracked pre-existing in the full-run report): the uniform 'DEV -- a development engine. Low stakes.' badge undercuts the mission's own 'production' framing, but this is the fixture-staging failure (R-SAFE-03 row), not a message-copy defect in isolation.
